title: Grastidium clausum
taxonomy: Grastidium clausum is classified within the family Restionaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as restio plants or rush-like plants. This family is further classified under the order Poales, a group of monocots that are primarily grass-like or sedge-like in appearance. Restionaceae family is native to the Southern Hemisphere, particularly found in Africa and Australia, with some species also found in New Zealand and South America. As a member of this family, Grastidium clausum shares characteristics with other restio plants, such as its narrow, grass-like leaves and small, white flowers. The classification of Grastidium clausum reflects its evolutionary relationship to other plants within the Restionaceae family.
